_meta.description: PJM reference data: glossary, pricing nodes, hubs, zones, interfaces

glossary._schema: Object mapping term -> { definition: string, ref: string, term?: string }

5CP.definition: The unrestricted load of a zone, LSE, or end-use customer, coincident with one of the five highest loads used in weather normalization of the PJM seasonal peak.

AC.definition: An alternating current is the movement of an electric charge that periodically reverses direction, compared to direct current which moves in only one direction. Electric power is delivered to homes and businesses through alternating current.

ACE.definition: A measure of the imbalance between sources of power and uses of power within the PJM RTO. The ACE calculation uses the difference between scheduled and actual net interchange plus the PJM RTO frequency bias contribution.

AFC.definition: A measure of the flow capability remaining on a Flowgate for further commercial activity over and above already committed uses. Defined as TFC less ETC, less CBM, less TRM, plus Postbacks and Counterflows.

AGC.definition: Equipment that automatically adjusts a Control Area's generation to maintain its interchange schedule plus its share of frequency regulation.

ARR.definition: Entitlements allocated annually to Firm Transmission Service Customers that entitle the holder to receive an allocation of the revenues from the Annual FTR Auction.

ATC.definition: The amount of energy above base case conditions that can be transferred reliably from one area to another over all transmission facilities without violating any pre- or post-contingency criteria.

Actual Load.definition: The Load Serving Entity's total load per Electric Distribution Company zone, as determined through meter readings and EDC load profiling methods.

Adjacent System or Adjacent Control Area.definition: Any control area directly interconnected with another control area.
Adjacent System or Adjacent Control Area.term: Adjacent System or Adjacent Control Area

Agent.definition: An entity appointed by a PJM member to do business in PJM on behalf of the member.

Aggregate.definition: A group of more than one individual bus into a pricing node (pnode) that is considered as a whole in the Energy Market and other various systems and Markets within PJM.

Analog Control.definition: A signal which, with respect to time, varies continuously in proportion to the measured quantity.

Ancillary Services.definition: Services necessary to support the transmission of energy. PJM operates several markets for ancillary services: the Synchronized Reserve Market, the Non-Synchronized Reserve Market, the Day-ahead Scheduling Reserve Market and the Regulation Market. There are also nonmarket ancillary services such as black start service and reactive service.

Annual Demand Resources.definition: Demand Resources which can be called on an unlimited number of times any day of the delivery year, unless on an approved maintenance outage.

Area Regulation Signal.definition: A signal generated by the PJM Control Center and sent to plants, stations and/or PJM members scheduled to provide regulation to change generation quickly to keep PJM's area control error within allowable limits.

BES.definition: Includes individual generation resources larger than 20 MVA or a generation plan with aggregate capacity greater than 75 MVA connected via step-up transformer(s) to facilities operated at voltages 100 kV or higher.

BRA.definition: The primary RPM auction held three years before the Delivery Year to procure capacity resources needed to meet the reliability requirement.

BTM.definition: Generation that is physically located behind the retail meter and does not participate in the wholesale market as a generation resource.

Balancing Authority.definition: The responsible entity that integrates resource plans ahead of time, maintains Demand and resource balance within a Balancing Authority Area, and supports Interconnection frequency in real time.

Balancing Authority Area.definition: The collection of generation, transmission, and loads within the metered boundaries of the Balancing Authority.

Balancing Energy Market.definition: Matches the power output of generators within the electric power system(s) and energy purchased from outside with the load within the system(s).

Balancing Operating Reserves.definition: Generation and demand response resources that operate as requested by PJM are guaranteed to recover the costs of their offer amounts through balancing operating reserve credits charged to market participants.

Base Offer Segment.definition: The buy or sell offer segment of a resource or virtual transaction that can consist of up to 10 MW/price pairs that are monotonically increasing.

Basepoint.definition: The desired megawatt output sent to a resource.

Bilateral Transaction.definition: An agreement between two entities (one or both being PJM Members) for the sale and delivery of a service.

Black Start Service.definition: Enables Transmission Providers and Transmission Owners to designate specific generators called Black Start Units whose location and capabilities are required to re-energize the transmission system following a system-wide blackout.

Black Start Unit.definition: A generating unit that has equipment enabling it to start without an outside electrical supply or a generating unit with a high operating factor with the demonstrated ability to automatically remain operating at reduced levels when disconnected from the grid.

Board of Managers.definition: The 10 members of the PJM Board of Managers responsible for maintaining PJM's independence and ensuring that PJM fulfills its business obligations and legal and regulatory requirements.

Bus.definition: A point of interconnection to the system where power produced becomes available for transmission. Also, an electrical conductor that serves as a common connection for two or more electrical circuits.

CBIR.definition: A group structured problem-solving process in which stakeholders develop and agree to support a proposal in the best interest of the whole.

CBM.definition: The portion of PJM's emergency import capability deducted from Total Transfer Capability to determine Available Transfer Capability. CBM is reserved to import capacity assistance from external areas under emergency conditions.

CC.definition: An electric generating technology in which electricity and process steam are produced from waste heat exiting from one or more combustion turbines, increasing the efficiency of the facility.

CEII.definition: Specific engineering, vulnerability, or detailed design information about proposed or existing critical infrastructure that relates to production, generation, transmission, or distribution of energy.

CETL.definition: The capability of the transmission system to support deliveries of electric energy to a given area experiencing a localized capacity emergency as determined in accordance with PJM Manuals.

CETO.definition: The amount of electric energy that a given area must be able to import to remain within a loss of load expectation of one event in 25 years during a localized capacity emergency.

CONE.definition: Averaged revenue required ($/MW-year) to build a reference combustion turbine in a specific area of PJM.

CT.definition: A generating unit in which a combustion turbine engine is the prime mover for an electrical generator. Typically used for peak shaving due to quick response capability.

CTR.definition: A method of allocating the economic value of transmission import capability into a constrained Locational Deliverability Area (LDA) to Load Serving Entities (LSEs).

CTS.definition: Interchange transactions between neighboring RTOs/ISOs designed to increase scheduling efficiencies across the seam using forward pricing information.

Capacitor.definition: An electronic component that stores an electric charge and releases it when required. When connected to the power system, capacitors provide reactive power and increase voltage.

Capacity.definition: System capacity is the total amount of electricity resources available to use if needed. PJM must have enough resources plus an additional reserve to supply the greatest amount of electricity used in one hour.

Capacity Factor.definition: The ratio of the total energy generated by a generating unit for a specified period to the maximum possible energy it could have generated at maximum capacity rating, expressed as a percent.

Capacity Interconnection Rights.definition: The rights to input generation as a Generation Capacity Resource into the Transmission System at the Point of Interconnection.

Capacity Performance Resource.definition: A generating unit, demand resource, or energy efficiency resource that has obligated itself to deliver electricity whenever PJM determines it is needed to meet power system emergencies.

Capacity Resource.definition: A generating unit, demand resource, energy efficiency resource or aggregate resource that has obligated itself to deliver electricity or reduce load whenever PJM determines it is needed.

Congestion.definition: A condition on the transmission system when one or more restrictions prevents the economic dispatch of electric energy from serving load.

Congestion Cost.definition: The price representing the inability to use the least expensive generation to meet electricity demand due to transmission limitations. One of the three components of LMP.

Contingency.definition: An event, usually involving the loss of one or more elements that affects the power system at least momentarily.

Contract Path.definition: A specific contiguous electrical path from a Point of Receipt to a Point of Delivery for which transfer rights have been contracted.

Control Area.definition: An electric power system or combination of systems bounded by interconnection metering and telemetry to which a common generation control scheme is applied. PJM operates as one control area.

Control Zone.definition: One or more transmission zones or multiple contiguous Zones as defined in PJM manuals.

Cost-Based Offers.definition: Offers that shall not exceed the variable cost of producing energy or service, as determined in accordance with the Cost Development Guidelines.

Curtailment.definition: A reduction in transmission service or generation as a result of system reliability conditions.

DAM.definition: A day-ahead hourly forward market in which PJM market participants may submit offers to sell and bids to buy energy. Results are posted daily by 1:30 p.m. and are financially binding.

DASR.definition: Voluntary, offer-based market for 30-minute (supplemental) reserves that can be provided by both Generation and Demand Resources, co-optimized with the Day-Ahead Energy Market.

DC.definition: The movement of an electric charge from negative to positive that flows in only one direction.

DCS.definition: A NERC standard measuring the ability of a control area to return Area Control Error either to zero or to its initial value following the loss of a large generating unit.

DEC.definition: An hourly bid, expressed in MWh, to purchase energy in the PJM Day-Ahead Energy Market if the Day-Ahead LMP is less than or equal to the specified bid price.

DFAX.definition: The percentage of power flowing on Element A that will be picked up or backed down on Element B as a result of an outage on Element A.

DR.definition: A resource with a demonstrated capability to provide a reduction in demand or otherwise control load.

DR Factor.definition: One of the parameters used to determine the Unforced Capacity value of demand resources in the capacity market.

Daily Unforced Capacity Obligation.definition: An LSE's daily assignment in megawatts of the Zonal Unforced Capacity Obligation.

Day-Ahead Demand.definition: The fixed and/or price-sensitive demand bids cleared in the PJM Day-Ahead Energy Market (financial hedge).

Delivery Year.definition: The 12 months beginning June 1 and extending through May 31 of the following year. Also referred to as Planning Year or Planning Period.

Demand.definition: The usage or consumption of electricity on a power system, generally expressed in kilowatt-hours or megawatt-hours.

Demand Response.definition: Program that allows end use customers to reduce their electricity usage during periods of higher power prices. Customers are compensated through Curtailment Service Providers.

Dispatch Rate.definition: The control signal, expressed in dollars per megawatt hour, calculated and transmitted continuously to direct the output level of all generation resources dispatched by PJM.

Distribution.definition: The final stage of delivering electricity to an end user. A distribution system steps down electricity from higher voltage levels on the transmission system.

Dynamic Schedule.definition: A telemetered value updated in real time used to adjust the interchange schedule in the AGC/ACE equation.

EDC.definition: A company that distributes electricity to end-use customers within a designated service territory.

EFOF.definition: The proportion of hours in a year that a unit is unavailable because of forced outages.

EFORd.definition: A measure of the probability that a generating unit will not be available due to forced outages or forced deratings when there is a demand on the unit to generate.

EMS.definition: Provides real-time monitoring of operational information for all critical electrical equipment, central to reliable operation of the bulk power system.

EWG.definition: Independent power facilities generating electricity for sale in wholesale power markets that do not meet Qualifying Facility requirements.

EcoMax.definition: The highest incremental MW output level a unit can achieve while following economic dispatch.

EcoMin.definition: The lowest MW output level a unit can achieve while following economic dispatch.

Economic Demand Response.definition: Ability for end-use customers to participate in wholesale energy and ancillary service markets through a Curtailment Service Provider when economic.

Economic Dispatch.definition: The short-term determination of the optimal output of generation facilities to meet system load at the lowest possible cost, subject to transmission and operational constraints.

EmerMax.definition: The maximum output of energy a generating unit can produce and still maintain a stable level of operation.

EmerMin.definition: The least amount of energy a unit can produce and still maintain a stable level of operation.

Emergency Demand Response.definition: A commitment to reduce load or consume electricity only up to a certain level when PJM needs assistance under expected emergency conditions (also called Load Management).

Energy.definition: The ability to do work. Electrical energy is produced by a generator and transmitted through the electric transmission and distribution systems. Measured in kilowatt-hours and megawatt-hours.

Energy Imbalance Service.definition: Used to supply energy for mismatch between scheduled delivery and actual loads that have occurred over an hour.

Energy Market.definition: A spot market in which energy is sold or purchased for immediate delivery. PJM's energy market functions at a wholesale level, establishing price by matching supply and demand.

Energy Market Opportunity Cost.definition: The value associated with an externally imposed environmental run-hour restriction on a generation unit.

Equivalent Load.definition: The sum of a Market Participant's net system requirements to serve its customer load in the PJM Region, plus its net bilateral transactions.

Exports.definition: The sum of all external transactions where PJM is the Point of Receipt.

External Resource.definition: A generation resource located outside the metered boundaries of the PJM RTO.

External Transaction.definition: An energy transaction between two parties in which the path of the energy crosses a PJM RTO border.

FMU.definition: A unit that was offer-capped for more than a defined proportion of its real-time run hours in the most recent 12-month period. FMU thresholds are 60%, 70% and 80%.

FPR.definition: The PJM installed reserve margin expressed in unforced capacity terms, applied to a peak load forecast to establish the required UCAP level.

FRR.definition: An alternative method for an eligible load-serving entity to meet a fixed resource requirement with its own capacity resources instead of RPM auctions.

FTR.definition: A financial instrument awarded to bidders in FTR Auctions that entitles the holder to a stream of revenues (or charges) based on the hourly Day Ahead congestion price differences across the path.

FTR Auction.definition: A PJM-administered market for FTRs in which participants may submit offers to sell and bids to buy on-peak, off-peak or 24 hour FTRs with terms of one month to three years.

Firm Transmission Service.definition: Transmission service intended to be available at all times to the maximum extent practicable, subject to emergency or unanticipated failure.

Frequency Bias.definition: The Balancing Authority's obligation to provide or absorb energy to assist in stabilizing frequency.

Hub.definition: A group of more than one individual bus into a regional pricing node (pnode) developed to produce a stable price signal in the Energy Market and other systems within PJM.

IA.definition: First, Second and Third Incremental Auctions allow capacity suppliers to purchase replacement capacity and PJM to adjust previously committed capacity levels.

IBT.definition: A transaction for the physical transfer of energy between two parties where the path remains inside the PJM RTO borders.

ICAP.definition: A MW value based on the summer net dependable capability of a unit and within the capacity interconnection right limits.

ICCP.definition: An industry standard protocol used to communicate real-time data between control centers.

IMM.definition: The Independent Market Monitor for PJM, established in 2008 by spinning off PJM's Market Monitoring Unit.

INC.definition: An hourly offer, expressed in MWh, to sell energy into the PJM Day-Ahead Energy Market if the Day-Ahead LMP is greater than or equal to the specified offer price.

IRM.definition: Percentage value used to establish the level of installed capacity resources providing an acceptable level of reliability.

IROL.definition: A System Operating Limit that, if violated, could lead to instability, uncontrolled separation, or cascading outages adversely impacting BES reliability.

Imports.definition: The sum of all external transactions where PJM is the Point of Delivery.

Inadvertent Interchange.definition: The difference between net actual energy flow and net scheduled energy flow into or out of a Control Area.

Incremental Cost.definition: The cost per MWh to produce all energy segments above the economic minimum level, calculated by summing each segment cost up to the generation level.

Interface.definition: A group of more than one individual bus into a proxy pricing node (pnode) developed for transactions into or out of the RTO.

LDA.definition: Sub-regions within PJM used in evaluating locational constraints. LDAs include transmission zones, sub-zones and combination of zones.

LMP.definition: The marginal price for energy at the location where energy is delivered or received. Expressed in $/MWh. LMP addresses Transmission System congestion and loss costs, as well as energy costs.

LOC.definition: Foregone revenues resulting from following PJM's dispatch instructions to provide some type of service or due to reliability or transmission constraints.

LOLE.definition: Defines the adequacy of capacity for the entire PJM footprint based on load exceeding available capacity, on average, only once in 10 years.

LSE.definition: Any entity that serves end-users within the PJM Control Area, granted authority or having an obligation pursuant to state or local law to sell electric energy to end-users.

Limited Demand Resources.definition: Demand Resources which can be called on during weekdays from noon to 8 p.m. up to 10 times from June through September.

Load.definition: The overall usage or consumption of electricity on a power supply, generally expressed in kilowatt-hours or megawatt-hours.

Load Curtailment.definition: Completely or partially reducing consumer electricity consumption.

Load Management.definition: A resource with a capacity commitment to reduce load when dispatched by PJM.

MCP.definition: The price paid by all load and paid to all suppliers for the service received or provided.

MOPR.definition: Provision that imposes a minimum offer screening process to determine whether an offer from a new resource is competitive and prevents artificially low offers in RPM Auctions.

MW.definition: A unit of power equaling one million watts. Under non-severe weather conditions, one MW could power roughly 800 to 1,000 average-sized American homes.

MWh.definition: A unit of measurement for the amount of electricity produced or consumed. Equal to 1 million watt-hours or 1,000 kilowatt-hours.

Marginal Losses Cost.definition: The price representing the power lost when power moves across the transmission system. One of the three components of LMP.

Member.definition: Any entity that has completed an application and satisfies the requirements of the PJM Operating Agreement to conduct business with PJM.

Mileage.definition: The summation of movement requested by the regulation control signal a resource is following. Calculated for the market hour and on a five minute basis for each regulation control signal.

NERC.definition: Operates as an electric reliability organization to improve the reliability and security of the bulk power system in North America through developing and enforcing reliability standards.

NITS.definition: Allows a Transmission Customer to integrate, plan, economically dispatch and regulate its network resources to serve its network load comparable to how the transmission provider serves its Native Load.

NSPL.definition: A load's contribution to the zone's metered annual peak load.

Non-Firm Point-to-Point Transmission Ser.definition: Point-to-Point Transmission Service reserved and scheduled on an as-available basis, subject to curtailment or interruption. Available for periods from one hour to one month.

OTDF.definition: The electric power transfer distribution factor with a specific system facility removed from service.

Obligation Peak Load.definition: The summation of the peak load contributions (PLCs) of end-use customers that an LSE is responsible to serve in a zone on an operating day.

Off Peak.definition: A period when consumers typically use less electricity: weekends, holidays, and weekdays from 11 p.m. to 7 a.m.

On-Peak.definition: A period when consumers typically use more electricity: weekdays from 7 a.m. to 11 p.m., except designated holidays.

Operating Day.definition: The daily 24-hour period beginning at midnight EPT for which transactions on the PJM Interchange Energy Market are scheduled.

Outage.definition: A short- or long-term loss of electric power to an area, or planned intervals when transmission lines or power plants are temporarily brought out of service for maintenance.

PAR.definition: A power system transformer with tap changing capability that can change the phase angle to increase or decrease power flow.

PBR.definition: PJM market structure aligning compensation with actual performance and benefits each resource provides to the power system.

PJM Control Center.definition: The equipment, facilities, and personnel used by PJM to coordinate and direct operation of the PJM RTO and administer the PJM Interchange Energy Market.

PJM Interchange Energy Market.definition: The regional competitive market administered by PJM for the purchase and sale of spot electric energy at wholesale in interstate commerce.

PJM RTO.definition: The control area recognized by NERC that coordinates wholesale electricity movement in all or parts of Delaware, Illinois, Indiana, Kentucky, Maryland, Michigan, New Jersey, North Carolina, Ohio, Pennsylvania, Tennessee, Virginia, West Virginia and the District of Columbia.

PJM Settlement, Inc..definition: A subsidiary of PJM providing member settlement transaction activities, acting as counterparty to members' pool transactions.

PLC.definition: An end-use customer's contribution to the zone's weather normalized summer peak load, as determined by the zone's EDC.

PRD.definition: End-use customers that commit to consume only a certain amount of electricity when energy prices are high, capping the amount of capacity required.

Pnode.definition: A single point or subset of points where a physical injection or withdrawal of energy is modeled and for which an LMP is calculated and used for financial settlements.

Point-to-Point Transmission Service.definition: The reservation and transmission of capacity and energy on either a firm or non-firm basis from Point(s) of Receipt to Point(s) of Delivery.

Primary Reserve.definition: Reserve capability that can be converted fully into energy within 10 minutes from PJM's request. Met with Synchronized Reserves and Non-Synchronized Reserves.

REG.definition: The capability of a resource with appropriate telecommunications and control to increase or decrease its output in response to a regulating control signal.

RMCP.definition: The price of supplying the last megawatt of Regulation needed to satisfy the requirement, calculated every five minutes through joint co-optimization.

RMR.definition: A generating unit slated for retirement but needed for reliability reasons, typically requested to remain operational beyond its proposed retirement date.

RPM.definition: PJM's capacity market design that includes a series of auctions to satisfy the reliability requirements for a Delivery Year.

RTM.definition: A balancing market in which clearing prices are calculated every five minutes based on actual system operations security-constrained economic dispatch.

Ramp Rate.definition: The rate, expressed in megawatts per minute, at which a generating unit can change output level.

Resource Clearing Price.definition: A locational and product-specific clearing price paid to a resource that clears in an RPM Auction.

Self-Scheduled Resource.definition: A generating resource turned on and committed into the energy market by the operating company. Also known as running for company.

Shadow Price.definition: The incremental reduction in congestion cost achieved by relieving a constraint by 1 MW.

Synchronized Reserve Market.definition: Real-time market for purchasing and selling Synchronized Reserve, with purpose of recovering Area Control Error after a resource loss or under frequency conditions. Resources must respond within 10 minutes and be electrically synchronized.

TO.definition: A member that owns or leases, with rights equivalent to ownership, Transmission Facilities.

Transmission Constraints.definition: Limitations on a transmission line or element that may be reached during normal or contingency system operations.

UCAP.definition: The MW value of a capacity resource in the PJM Capacity Market. For generating units, UCAP equals installed capacity multiplied by (1 - unit's EFORd).

UTC.definition: A bid in the Day-Ahead Market to purchase congestion and losses between two points, consisting of a source, sink, MW quantity and bid spread.

VAR.definition: The product of voltage and the out-of-phase component of alternating current. Produced by capacitors and overexcited generators, absorbed by reactors and inductive devices.

VRR.definition: An administratively determined demand curve defining prices for various levels of resource procurement used in RPM Auctions.

Zonal Capacity Price.definition: The price charged to an LSE for their unforced capacity obligation in a zone based on RPM Auction results.

Zone.definition: A transmission owner's area within the PJM Region.

nodes._schema: Array of [pnodeId: number, pnodeName: string, typeIndex: number, zone: string]. Types: 0=AGGREGATE, 1=EHV, 2=EXT, 3=GEN, 4=HUB, 5=INTERFACE, 6=LOAD, 7=RESIDUAL_METERED_EDC, 8=ZONE
